arguments.description: Disables unicast/global auto fix for BSSID MAC addresses.

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# proximoth -h
 Usage: proximoth [options] <target>
 
<target>                            : MAC address of the target.
 
 options:
 
-h, --help                          : Prints this screen.
 
-o <file>, --out-file <file>        : File to write statistics after shutdown.
 
-b <bssid>, --bssid <bssid>         : Custom BSSID to be injected as sender MAC address.
                                      Address is fixed automatically to be global and unicast.
 
-a, --no-mac-autofix                : Disables unicast/global auto fix for BSSID MAC addresses.
 
-i <iface>, --interface <iface>     : Wireless interface to use packet injection and sniffing.
                                      Obligatory option.
 
-d <file>, --dump-file <file>       : Write all CTS captures to a PCAP file.
 
-r <us>, --rts-interval <us>        : Microseconds as threshold to wait between RTS injections.
                                      Setting it to a low value might cause malfunction.
                                      Default: 500000
 
-t, --text-mode                     : Enables text only mode.
 
--version                           : Prints version number and author information.

local_help.man:
PROXIMOTH(1)                    Proximoth Manual                   PROXIMOTH(1)

NAME
     proximoth - Control Frame Attack Vulnerability Detection Tool

SYNOPSIS
     proximoth [options] <target>

DESCRIPTION
     Proximoth is a command-line tool to detect proximal privacy violation vul-
     nerability  in  wireless chipsets with Control Frame Attack. This vulnera-
     bility lets an attacker detect the presence of  the  Wi-Fi  device  within
     range  by  using  the global MAC address of it. Proximoth basically repli-
     cates this attack to achieve this goal.

     Proximoth does not discover the global MAC address of the target,  but  it
     assumes  that you already know it or gathered it before.  The expected be-
     haviour from a vulnerable device is to respond with a CTS  frame  when  an
     RTS frame was sent to its global MAC address.

     <target> is the global MAC address of the target Wi-Fi device.

DISCLAIMER
     This software is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but with-
     out a warranty.  No responsibility will be taken for any illegal activity,
     use it at your own risk.

OPTIONS
     -o <file>, --out-file <file>
             File to write statistics after shutdown.

     -b <bssid>, --bssid <bssid>
             Custom BSSID to be injected as sender MAC address.
             Address is fixed automatically to be global and unicast.

     -a, --no-mac-autofix
             Disables unicast/global auto fix for BSSID MAC addresses.

     -i <iface>, --interface <iface>
             Wireless interface to use packet injection and sniffing.
             Obligatory option.

     -d <file>, --dump-file <file>
             Write all CTS captures to a PCAP file.

     -r <us>, --rts-interval <us>
             Microseconds as threshold to wait between RTS injections.
             Setting it to a low value might cause malfunction.
             Default: 500000

     -t, --text-mode
             Enables text only mode.

     --version
             Prints version number and author information.

EXAMPLES
     $ sudo proximoth -i wlan0 10:DA:41:E8:FA:DC

     $  sudo  proximoth  -i wlan0 10:DA:41:E8:FA:DC --dump-file dump --out-file
     record

     $ sudo proximoth -i wlan0 10:DA:41:E8:FA:DC --rts-interval 100000

     $ sudo proximoth -i wlan0 10:DA:41:E8:FA:DC -b 1C:CC:D6:00:00:00

SEE ALSO
     https://www.researchgate.net/publication/314361145_A_Study_of_MAC_Ad-
     dress_Randomization_in_Mobile_Devices_and_When_it_Fails

     https://technicaluserx.wordpress.com/2022/07/22/proximoth

1.0.0                              21/10/2023                      PROXIMOTH(1)
local_help.apropos: proximoth (1)        - Control Frame Attack Vulnerability Detection Tool

apt_metadata.description:
Control Frame Attack Vulnerability Detection Tool
Proximoth is a command-line tool to detect Wi-Fi
devices in proximity with Control Frame Attack.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use proximoth because proximoth - Control Frame Attack Vulnerability Detection Tool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `proximoth [options] <target>` as documented upstream for proximoth.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo proximoth -i wlan0 10:DA:41:E8:FA:DC` as documented upstream for proximoth.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo proximoth -i wlan0 10:DA:41:E8:FA:DC --dump-file dump --out-file record` as documented upstream for proximoth.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `sudo proximoth -i wlan0 10:DA:41:E8:FA:DC --rts-interval 100000` as documented upstream for proximoth.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair proximoth with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `proximoth --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
